initial_program:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Output cell definition
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Exactly one color per output cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Copy for bottom half (keep original copy)  only for rows r >= dr
copy(R, C, V) :- input(R0, C0, V), V != 5, R0 >= (N / 2), R = R0, C = C0.

% Copy for top half  shift down by dr rows and shift columns by floor(M/2)
copy(R, C, V) :- input(R0, C0, V), V != 5, R0 < (N / 2),
                 (R0 + (N / 2)) \ N = R,
                 (C0 + (M / 2)) \ M = C.

% Copy for bottom half  shift up by dr rows and shift columns by (M/2) - (M \ 2)
copy(R, C, V) :- input(R0, C0, V), V != 5,
                 (R0 + (N / 2)) \ N = R,
                 (C0 + ((M / 2) - (M \ 2))) \ M = C.

% Derive the output from the copy relations
output(R, C, V) :- copy(R, C, V).

% Ensure cells that are not produced by a copy must be zero
:- output(R, C, V), not copy(R, C, V), V != 0.

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Output cell definition
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Exactly one color per output cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Copy for bottom half (keep original copy)  only for rows r >= dr
copy(R, C, V) :- input(R0, C0, V), V != 5, R0 >= (N / 2), R = R0, C = C0.

% Copy for top half  shift down by dr rows and shift columns by floor(M/2)
copy(R, C, V) :- input(R0, C0, V), V != 5, R0 < (N / 2),
                 (R0 + (N / 2)) \ N = R,
                 (C0 + (M / 2)) \ M = C.

% Copy for bottom half  shift up by dr rows and shift columns by (M/2) - (M \ 2)
copy(R, C, V) :- input(R0, C0, V), V != 5,
                 (R0 + (N / 2)) \ N = R,
                 (C0 + ((M / 2) - (M \ 2))) \ M = C.

% Derive the output from the copy relations
output(R, C, V) :- copy(R, C, V).

% Ensure cells that are not produced by a copy must be zero
:- output(R, C, V), not copy(R, C, V), V != 0.

#show output/3.

% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% Output cell definition
output_cell(R, C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Exactly one color per output cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Copy for bottom half (keep original copy)  only for rows r >= dr
copy(R, C, V) :- input(R0, C0, V), V != 5, R0 >= (N / 2), R = R0, C = C0.

% Copy for top half  shift down by dr rows and shift columns by floor(M/2)
copy(R, C, V) :- input(R0, C0, V), V != 5, R0 < (N / 2),
                 (R0 + (N / 2)) \ N = R,
                 (C0 + (M / 2)) \ M = C.

% Copy for bottom half  shift up by dr rows and shift columns by (M/2) - (M \ 2)
copy(R, C, V) :- input(R0, C0, V), V != 5,
                 (R0 + (N / 2)) \ N = R,
                 (C0 + ((M / 2) - (M \ 2))) \ M = C.

% Derive the output from the copy relations
output(R, C, V) :- copy(R, C, V).

% Ensure cells that are not produced by a copy must be zero
:- output(R, C, V), not copy(R, C, V), V != 0.

#show output/3.
